title: Meridian UK Structured Notes Series A
content:
MERIDIAN UK STRUCTURED NOTES SERIES A - PRODUCT FACTSHEET

This factsheet provides essential information for senior advisors regarding the Meridian UK Structured Notes Series A, designed for sophisticated investors seeking enhanced returns through capital market exposure with principal protection features.

PRODUCT OVERVIEW

The Meridian UK Structured Notes Series A are five-year capital-protected notes linked to the performance of the FTSE 100 Index, with a participation rate of 125% on positive performance. The notes offer 100% capital protection at maturity, provided the underlying index does not breach the 65% American barrier during the investment term. Maximum potential return is capped at 85% of initial investment over the five-year period.

Minimum investment: £50,000
Maximum investment: £2,500,000 per client
Issue price: 100% of nominal value
Annual management charge: 1.25%
Structuring fee: 2.75% (deducted at inception)

RISK CONSIDERATIONS

In accordance with FCA COBS 9.2 requirements, clients must be made aware that despite capital protection features, the notes carry significant risks. The American barrier provision means that if the FTSE 100 Index closes below 65% of its initial level on any business day during the investment term, capital protection is forfeited and clients may experience losses equivalent to the underlying index performance.

Counterparty risk exposure exists with Deutsche Bank AG as the note issuer (current S&P rating: A-). In the event of issuer default, investors rank as unsecured creditors and may recover significantly less than their initial investment, regardless of underlying index performance.

Liquidity risk is substantial as no secondary market exists. Early redemption is possible only at the issuer's discretion and may result in significant losses due to mark-to-market valuation adjustments and prevailing interest rate conditions.

REGULATORY COMPLIANCE

These structured notes fall under the PRIIPs Regulation framework, requiring provision of the Key Information Document (KID) to all prospective investors before investment execution. The KID contains standardized risk and performance scenarios calculated using the PRIIPs methodology.

Under FCA COBS 9.2 suitability requirements, these notes are classified as complex instruments suitable only for clients with demonstrated understanding of structured product mechanics and barrier features. Advisors must conduct enhanced suitability assessments, documenting client knowledge of American barrier risks and potential loss scenarios.

TARGET MARKET

Appropriate for high-net-worth individuals with investment portfolios exceeding £500,000, seeking equity market exposure with downside protection within a diversified portfolio allocation. Clients should have previous structured product experience and capacity to withstand potential total loss of invested capital.

Not suitable for clients requiring immediate liquidity access, those with limited structured product knowledge, or where the investment represents more than 15% of total investable assets.

PERFORMANCE SCENARIOS

Based on current market conditions and PRIIPs standardized methodology:
- Stress scenario: -100% (barrier breach with significant index decline)
- Unfavorable scenario: 0% (barrier intact, negative index performance)
- Moderate scenario: +31.25% over five years
- Favorable scenario: +85% over five years (maximum return achieved)

TAXATION

Structured notes are subject to capital gains tax treatment for UK residents. Annual tax on chargeable event gains may apply for higher-rate taxpayers under the offshore fund rules. Professional tax advice is recommended given the complex nature of structured product taxation.

This product requires comprehensive suitability documentation and is restricted to senior advisor distribution only. All investments must be supported by detailed rationale addressing client objectives, risk tolerance, and portfolio diversification requirements.
